items.title: France 'best prepared' in Europe for heatwaves, while UK views heat as 'occasional inconvenience'
items.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Ian Williams, Professor of Applied Environmental Science in the Faculty of Engineering and Physical Sciences at the University of Southampton, UK. He argues that Europe's current heatwave should not be understood as an exceptional weather event but as evidence of a new climatic reality shaped by anthropogenic warming. While acknowledging that adaptation measures can significantly reduce mortality and social disruption, he maintains that increasingly intense heat extremes are ultimately driven by the accumulation of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere. Williams presents France as a notable case study in climate adaptation, citing its post-2003 reforms as among the most comprehensive heat governance frameworks in Europe. However, he also stresses that even the best-prepared societies face mounting challenges as climate change amplifies the frequency and intensity of extreme heat events.

items.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
items.description: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

items.title: 'Heartbreaking': Sydney boarding house to be turned into luxury flats
items.description: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

items.title: EU moves to strengthen Europol against rising digital, cross-border crime
Rubio assures Gulf allies US will protect interests in Iran peace talks
items.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Wednesday assured Gulf allies that Washington would protect their interests as it pursues a final settlement with Iran. President Donald Trump said talks were progressing well, while requesting nearly $88 billion in supplemental funding for the Pentagon after Congress passed a resolution urging an end to hostilities.

items.title: Arab League confirms Egyptian diplomat Nabil Fahmy as secretary general
items.description: The Arab League on Monday confirmed Egyptian veteran diplomat Nabil Fahmy as its new chief during a meeting of foreign ministers in Amman, a statement said.

items.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
items.description: Experts say Trump is likely to ignore it, but that even though it carries little power, it is politically significant.

items.title: North Korea should build two warships a year in next five years, Kim says
items.description: The ⁠Navy had been the weakest part of North Korea’s military forces, Mr. Kim said, ‌adding that its capabilities would now be “something incredible beyond imagination

items.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
items.description: An officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.

items.title: Nairobi signs $1.2 billion airport expansion deal with China Road and Bridge Corp.
items.description: Kenya has signed a $1.2 billion deal with China Road and Bridge Corporation to expand Nairobi’s Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, the country’s transport minister said on Tuesday.

items.title: US NTSB to investigate fatal Texas Tesla crash
Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries
items.description: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

items.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
items.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

items.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
items.description: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

items.title: America’s Mamdani moment
items.description: Zohran Mamdani’s victory in New York, a city with a budget of over $100 billion and a population larger than that of 40 U.S. States, demonstrates that the political movement has transcended niche activism to achieve mainstream electoral viability

items.title: Djed Spence's Thomas Partey handshake snub sparks World Cup debate
items.description: England defender Djed Spence appeared to be the only player in the squad who did not shake Thomas Partey's hand before England's World Cup clash with Ghana in Boston.

items.title: PACOM, the deeper meaning behind a dropped prefix
items.description: The U.S.’s reversion from INDOPACOM to PACOM suggests that its regional shifts run deeper than a simple name change

items.title: A U.S.-Iran dispute over nuclear inspections clouds work to finalize a war-ending deal
items.description: As U.S.-Iran talks continued, a break in the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be in the works.

items.title: Southern Lebanon's displaced Shiites fear return of old divisions
items.description: Israel has ordered Shiite-majority communities to be emptied, accusing residents of supporting Hezbollah, while allowing Christian, Sunni and other residents to remain. That risks aggravating sectarian tensions that have been mostly dormant since Lebanon's 1982-2000 civil war.

items.title: DR Congo fans priced out as Leopards shirt costs surge
items.description: Excitement surrounding the Democratic Republic of Congo's national football team, which has qualified for the 2026 World Cup, is being overshadowed by a sharp rise in jersey prices in the country's east, where supporters say the cost of showing their support has become unaffordable.

items.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
items.description: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

items.title: U.N. nuclear agency boss signals that inspectors will visit Iran’s nuclear sites
items.description: The head of the U.N.’s nuclear agency has signalled that Iranian nuclear enrichment sites would be visited by his inspectors, a key component in the interim deal between the United States and Iran to reach an end to the war

items.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
items.description: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Blood ties and opportunity: Why more World Cup players than ever are not representing their birth nations
items.description: Nearly a quarter of footballers at the 2026 tournament are not playing for their country of birth.

items.title: South African police probe killing of Malawian amid rising anti-immigrant attacks
items.description: The 29-year-old man died during a march against illegal immigrants at the Jika Joe Informal Settlement in Pietermaritzburg.

items.title: US public health agency concludes hantavirus response as outbreak eases
items.description: The US Department of Health and Human Services has announced the end of the monitoring period for the virus.

items.title: Modigliani nude sets European auction record at London art sale
items.description: A London auction of artworks from billionaire Joe Lewis's private collection raised $392.6 million on Wednesday, led by an Amedeo Modigliani nude portrait that set a European auction record. The first sale featured 48 works by artists including Pablo Picasso, René Magritte and Gustav Klimt. Lewis, former majority owner of Tottenham Hotspur, transferred his stake to a family trust in 2022.

items.title: Longer, hotter heatwaves leading to rise in heat stress around the world
items.description: Heatwaves around the world are getting hotter and lasting longer according to a new study. Scientists analysed a global dataset of human heat stress and found some subtropical countries are experiencing 50 more days per year of at least strong heat stress compared with the 1970s.

items.title: World Cup: Why Mexico may be LA's favorite team
items.description: Mexican-American football fans in the USA often split allegiances between their two cultures. For many in Los Angeles, the Mexico national team trumps the nation they call home.

items.title: Schweinsteiger's Ivory Coast comments spark racism debate
items.description: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

items.title: New dollar-cash videos put Insaurralde case back into spotlight
items.description: Videos allegedly showing hidden dollar stashes surface from Martín Insaurralde case; Footage leaked by 'La Nación' shows Jesica Cirio exposing secret stashes kept by veteran Peronist. Leer más

items.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
items.description: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

items.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
items.description: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

items.title: India, U.S. note ‘substantial progress’ on trade deal talks as U.S. Trade Representative Greer ends two-day visit to Delhi
items.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer held multiple meetings with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal; however, the two sides have not yet been able to announce a deadline by which the trade deals will be finalised

items.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
items.description: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

items.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
items.description: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.
